message:
Add NTFS watcher and index subsystem stubs

Scaffold the core indexing pipeline crates per PLAN §3 (NTFS integration), §4 (metadata index),
§5 (content index), §6 (extraction), §7 (scheduler). Minimal placeholder modules establish
the dependency graph and module boundaries.

## ntfs-watcher (§3: NTFS integration and change tracking)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Volume discovery via GetLogicalDrives + GetVolumeInformationW §3.1
- MFT enumeration using usn-journal-rs iterators §3.2
- USN journal tailing with gap detection and journal_id validation §3.3
- Emit FileEvent stream (Created, Deleted, Modified, Renamed) to scheduler
- Maintain lightweight LRU path cache + string interner §3.4

Design notes:
- No in-memory directory tree; rely on usn-journal-rs path resolution
- Handle journal wrap by detecting journal_id changes and triggering rescan §3.3
- FRN -> DocKey conversion via VolumeId mapping table

## meta-index (§4: Metadata and filename index)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Tantivy schema for filename/path/ext/size/timestamps/flags §4.1
- IndexWriter for initial MFT build with periodic commits §4.2
- IndexReader with tuned cache for low-latency name queries §4.1
- Query path: prefix queries, term queries, boolean filters §4.3
- Optional FST accelerator for ultra-fast prefix lookup §4.4

Schema fields:
- doc_key (u64 FAST + STORED), volume (u16 FAST), name (TEXT), path (TEXT),
  ext (STRING), size (u64 FAST), created/modified (i64 FAST), flags (u32 FAST)

Tokenizers:
- Name: split on [\, /, '.', '-', '_'] for Everything-like prefix matching
- Path: directory separator tokenization
- Ext: raw keyword

## content-index (§5: Full-text content indexing)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Separate Tantivy index for content field §5.1
- Schema: doc_key, volume, name, path, ext, size, modified, content_lang, content (TEXT) §5.2
- IndexWriter tuning: heap_size 64-256 MB, 2-4 threads, LogMergePolicy for bounded merges §5.3
- Query orchestrator: parse AST, build BooleanQuery, merge meta+content results §5.4
- Ranking: BM25 + manual boosts (name match > prefix, recency) §5.4

Design rationale:
- Separate index: Allows independent compaction, per-volume content policies, and optional disable
- Worker-only writer: Service never holds content IndexWriter to stay tiny §5.3

## content-extractor (§6: Content extraction subsystem)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Extractous integration for 50+ formats (PDF, Office, HTML, Markdown, EPUB) §6.2
- Optional IFilter COM bridge for proprietary formats (feature-gated) §6.3
- Optional Tesseract OCR for scanned PDFs/images §6.4
- ComponentManager: Auto-download/install Tesseract binaries to private dir §6.5
- Streaming extraction with max_bytes_per_file, max_chars limits §6.6

Extractor stack pattern:
1. Try Extractous (primary, fastest)
2. Fallback to IFilter if enabled and format unsupported
3. If image-only doc detected, run OCR up to ocr_max_pages

Resource limits (from ExtractSection config):
- max_bytes_per_file: 16 MiB (prevent huge memory spikes)
- max_chars: 200k (Tantivy doc cap)
- Archive recursion depth limit (to be defined)

## semantic-index (future: §ADVANCED_FEATURES.md hybrid semantic search)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Vector embedding index (separate from Tantivy)
- Hybrid lexical + semantic query fusion
- Model: all-minilm-l12-v2 or similar small transformer

Status: Stub only; disabled by default (FeaturesSection.semantic_search = false)

## scheduler (§7: Scheduler & background execution)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Idle detection: GetLastInputInfo + GetTickCount64 state machine §7.1
  - States: Active (<15s), WarmIdle (15-60s), DeepIdle (>60s)
- System load sampling: sysinfo CPU/disk thresholds §7.2
- Job categories: Critical (cheap, always), Metadata (WarmIdle+), Content (DeepIdle+) §7.3
- Worker spawning: Batch content jobs, invoke index-worker binary, monitor exit §7.4
- Priority management: Service NORMAL_PRIORITY, worker BELOW_NORMAL/IDLE §7.4

Job queue implementation (to be added):
- crossbeam-channel MPSC per category
- Per-tick budget (N files or M MB)
- Backlog tracking and exponential backoff on worker failures

## Status
All modules are placeholder stubs (empty or minimal lib.rs). Establishes:
- Crate boundaries matching PLAN architecture
- Dependency flow: ntfs-watcher → scheduler → index-worker → {meta,content}-index
- Module responsibilities documented for parallel agent implementation

Next steps:
- Service + IPC infrastructure (c00.2.2-c00.2.4)
- NTFS watcher implementation (c00.3.x)
- Metadata index build (c00.4.x)

Related: PLAN §1.2 (workspace layout), §3-7 (subsystem specs), AGENTS.md (MCP coordination)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,118 @@
+//! NTFS integration layer: volume discovery, MFT enumeration, and USN tailing.
+//!
+//! This crate intentionally keeps a small surface: pure data types and trait
+//! contracts that the service can implement with platform-specific code under
+//! `windows`/`windows-sys`. The goal for c00.3 is to have a compilable,
+//! testable scaffold that mirrors the implementation plan without yet wiring
+//! Win32 calls.
+
+use core_types::{DocKey, VolumeId};
+use thiserror::Error;
+
+pub type Usn = u64;
+
+/// Static information about a mounted NTFS volume.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+pub struct VolumeInfo {
+    /// Small runtime identifier assigned by the service.
+    pub id: VolumeId,
+    /// Volume GUID path such as `\\?\Volume{...}\`.
+    pub guid_path: String,
+    /// Optional drive letters currently mapped to the volume.
+    pub drive_letters: Vec<char>,
+}
+
+/// Lightweight metadata for a single file or directory.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+pub struct FileMeta {
+    pub doc_key: DocKey,
+    pub name: String,
+    pub is_dir: bool,
+    pub size: u64,
+}
+
+/// Stream of logical file-system events derived from the USN journal.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
+pub enum FileEvent {
+    Created(FileMeta),
+    Deleted(DocKey),
+    Modified { doc: DocKey },
+    Renamed { from: DocKey, to: FileMeta },
+    AttributesChanged { doc: DocKey },
+}
+
+/// Cursor for resuming USN processing.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
+pub struct JournalCursor {
+    pub last_usn: Usn,
+    pub journal_id: u64,
+}
+
+/// Errors that can surface while interacting with NTFS / USN APIs.
+#[derive(Debug, Error)]
+pub enum NtfsError {
+    #[error("volume discovery failed: {0}")]
+    Discovery(String),
+    #[error("usn journal error: {0}")]
+    Journal(String),
+    #[error("mft enumeration failed: {0}")]
+    Mft(String),
+    #[error("io error: {0}")]
+    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
+}
+
+/// Trait abstraction to make the platform-specific implementation swap-able in tests.
+pub trait NtfsWatcher {
+    /// Discover NTFS volumes.
+    fn discover_volumes(&self) -> Result<Vec<VolumeInfo>, NtfsError>;
+
+    /// Enumerate the MFT and stream file metadata snapshots.
+    fn enumerate_mft(&self, volume: &VolumeInfo) -> Result<Vec<FileMeta>, NtfsError>;
+
+    /// Tail the USN journal starting at the given cursor.
+    fn tail_usn(
+        &self,
+        volume: &VolumeInfo,
+        cursor: JournalCursor,
+    ) -> Result<(Vec<FileEvent>, JournalCursor), NtfsError>;
+}
+
+/// Discover NTFS volumes available on the machine.
+///
+/// In the scaffold this returns an empty list; the concrete implementation will
+/// call Win32 APIs (GetLogicalDrives, GetVolumeInformationW, etc.).
+pub fn discover_volumes() -> Result<Vec<VolumeInfo>, NtfsError> {
+    // TODO: implement volume discovery via Win32 APIs (windows / windows-sys).
+    Ok(Vec::new())
+}
+
+/// Enumerate the MFT for a given volume and emit file metadata snapshots.
+///
+/// A production implementation will stream records to avoid large memory
+/// spikes; here we surface the contract only.
+pub fn enumerate_mft(_volume: &VolumeInfo) -> Result<Vec<FileMeta>, NtfsError> {
+    // TODO: plumb usn-journal-rs MFT iterator and resolve parent/name into paths.
+    Ok(Vec::new())
+}
+
+/// Tail the USN journal for a volume and emit file events from the given cursor.
+pub fn tail_usn(
+    _volume: &VolumeInfo,
+    _cursor: JournalCursor,
+) -> Result<(Vec<FileEvent>, JournalCursor), NtfsError> {
+    // TODO: connect to USN journal, read deltas, and return next cursor.
+    Ok((Vec::new(), _cursor))
+}
+
+#[cfg(test)]
+mod tests {
+    use super::*;
+
+    #[test]
+    fn doc_key_round_trip() {
+        let doc = DocKey::from_parts(42, 1_234_567_890);
+        let (vol, frn) = doc.into_parts();
+        assert_eq!(vol, 42);
+        assert_eq!(frn, 1_234_567_890);
+    }
+}
